title: The buildings have symbolism
wikitext:
[]: The buildings have symbolism?             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Wed Jul 28, 2010 12:30 pm So I was on wiki reading up on Eva for the ten billionth time and some where it mentioned that in the analysis of the series, the fans(us) claim that the buildings of tokyo3 had some symbolism(even in the first episode). Not that I doubt this because it's Eva; I was wondering if someone could enlighten me on this.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Jul 28, 2010 12:41 pm Maybe it could mean how Shinji has to come out of his shell to connect with people, like the buildings rise out of the ground? Or that they "run away" whenever danger comes.

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Wed Jul 28, 2010 1:39 pm O ya, I tottaly forgot. And even when they do hide, some still get damaged in some battles. I don't know if I should rewarch those episodes, not in the mood to over analyze today.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Wed Jul 28, 2010 1:42 pm That's more like a metaphor than an actual symbol.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Jul 28, 2010 2:48 pm konfunoodle wrote: O ya, I tottaly forgot. And even when they do hide, some still get damaged in some battles. I don't know if I should rewarch those episodes, not in the mood to over analyze today.

[]:             Quote Post by Tycho » Wed Jul 28, 2010 6:31 pm Hah, I think the buildings (Tokyo3 as whole) are more of a graphical representation (metaphor?) of how everyone gets shredded to pieces mentally. But I guess it could be that yeah, when "danger" (angels for the building, contact with others for people) arises, they retract into themselves. When everything is peaceful (no angel threat, no pain from interacting with people), they reach out beautifully... And then, BAM! cross shaped gianormous asplosion shrecks it all to heck, whether you have withdrawn or not.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 29, 2010 8:45 am So within that context, what does it mean when all of the buildings were destroyed in episode 23?

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Thu Jul 29, 2010 12:51 pm It is showing Shinji's mental health at this point of the series and now with Ayanami gone, Asuka in the hospital, and his fear of Misato, there is no one for him to make contact with. I'm pulling this out of butt btw.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Jul 29, 2010 10:30 pm I've never heard about the buildings being symbols OR metaphors... I dunno, we know everything's a penis in the series the buildings can "grow". Oz wrote: That's more like a metaphor than an actual symbol.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 30, 2010 10:11 am konfunoodle wrote: It is showing Shinji's mental health at this point of the series and now with Ayanami gone, Asuka in the hospital, and his fear of Misato, there is no one for him to make contact with.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Jul 30, 2010 12:53 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: That actually makes sense. Because that sky is so friggin' sexy. And those angels are such bone killers. In fact, anything involving mommy and daddy (Lillith and Adam) fighting is a bone killer, really. Actually that metaphor, whereas it was the first thing I thought of, really doesn't make any sense at all within context of NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Jul 30, 2010 2:43 pm A Freudian show must have phallic symbols.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 30, 2010 7:39 pm TehDonutKing wrote: YOU KNOW NOTHING ABOUT SYMBOLISM. IF IT'S TALLER THAN IT IS WIDE, IT'S A PENIS.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Aug 02, 2010 3:48 pm Well, you guys beat me to the "It's a phallic symbol, lolz" line, however... These buildings slide in and out of the ground. Food for thought?

[]:             Quote Post by Hoshikawa-kun » Wed Aug 04, 2010 12:21 am The buildings retract at the sight of danger just as Shinji retracts from others; they rise up only when the danger, and others, are gone. When Shinji's emotional state is destroyed, the buildings are destroyed. There's a term in literature for this, one I really dislike because it's overused, but it's called 'Psychological landscape', where the scenery reflects the characters. Aside from this though, I noticed something going through the wiki's episode 4 summary. When Shinji runs away, while going through the city he hallucinates that the buildings are closing in on him, and he subsequently runs to the freedom of the country side. This may be more symbolic than the usual fair, as it could represent Misato(what he's running from) being oppressive to Shinji, and in-story it could be some form of panic attack brought on by Misato getting extremely close to Shinji previously, both in terms of living conditions and physically. She presses herself right up to him in episode 1, grabs him by the head and sees him fully exposed in episode 2, enters his room without waiting for an answer as to whether or not she's welcome, and overall appears highly overbearing and invading to someone who previously had lived an isolated life with his teacher. Actually, this shot of a building from episode 4 is quite interesting when compared to another recurring shot which is highlighted by what Shinji encounters in the countryside that same episode A city building that in the current deliberately odd lighting looks like Misato's black shirt flanked by her jacket, right after Shinji ran away from Misato, and followed up with a crossroads that looks like a cross hearkening back to the necklace Misato wears? And the entire shot it's referencing is considered important and is referenced again in EoE? ...Or it's just my silly guessing.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 04, 2010 12:27 am ^ I remember making a similar comment on the ep. 4 commentary. That whole scene where Shinji can be seen framed against that red background with the buildings does seem to by stressing their significance.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:57 am Hoshikawa-kun wrote: which is highlighted by what Shinji encounters in the countryside that same episode

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:43 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: ^ I remember making a similar comment on the ep. 4 commentary. That whole scene where Shinji can be seen framed against that red background with the buildings does seem to by stressing their significance.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Aug 14, 2010 9:03 am Phallic references are bullshit. If you look hard enough, they´re EVERYWHERE: Eva 01´s Horn Invasive/Penetrating Angel that looks like a tentacle Ramiel´s Drill that penetrates the geo front . The Lance of Long inus Bakelite flooding(Semen lol) etc. And the list goes on and on...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Aug 14, 2010 10:51 pm slothen wrote: oh how i miss the audio commentary.
